metaTitle: Square Root Curve Calculator – Instant Grade Curve
metaDescription: Use our square root curve calculator to instantly curve any test score. Formula: √Score × 10. Includes chart, Excel method, and grade lookup table.
keywords: square root curve calculator, grade curve calculator, test score curve, square root grading formula, curve grades, grade lookup table, excel grade curve
pageDescription: Square root curve = √(Score) × 10, for example if score is 64 then √64 = 8 and 8 × 10 = 80.

mainEntity.name: What is a square root curve?

acceptedAnswer.text: A square root curve is a grading method where the square root of a score is taken and multiplied by 10 to adjust grades, usually boosting lower scores more.

mainEntity.name: How do you calculate a square root curve?

acceptedAnswer.text: Take the square root of the original score and multiply it by 10. Example: √64 × 10 = 80.

mainEntity.name: What is the formula for a square root curve?
Does a square root curve always increase scores?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, most scores between 1 and 99 increase, while 0 and 100 remain unchanged.

mainEntity.name: What is 70 on a square root curve?
